result.preview: "I\u2019m unable to reliably compute per\u2011state case closure time from the available databases: the support.Case date fields are stored as text in PostgreSQL and require casting/cleanup logic that isn\u2019t supported in this environment, and there\u2019s no direct, joinable state/region field on cases themselves without cross\u2011database joins that also aren\u2019t supported in Postgres.\n\nBecause of these technical constraints, I can\u2019t confidently identify which specific state has the quickest case closure time over the past 6 quarters."

args.answer:
I’m unable to reliably compute per‑state case closure time from the available databases: the support.Case date fields are stored as text in PostgreSQL and require casting/cleanup logic that isn’t supported in this environment, and there’s no direct, joinable state/region field on cases themselves without cross‑database joins that also aren’t supported in Postgres.

Because of these technical constraints, I can’t confidently identify which specific state has the quickest case closure time over the past 6 quarters.
